content.full_text:
June 17, 1992

FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
The Travelers Business Center
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814

RE: REQUEST FOR ADVICE

Dear Sir or Madam:

A Simi Valley Council Member operates a small business within the City as a sole proprietorship. The clientele of the business does NOT meet or exceed the "public generally" thresholds in 2 CCR 18703.5. Among the clientele of the business are developers, consultants or agents of developers, and City employees. Some of these individuals have made purchases exceeding $250.00 during the past 12 months.

Questions:

1. If a developer whose past 12 months purchases from the Council Member exceed $250.00 submits to the City Council a development proposal which will have a material financial effect upon the developer's income, assets or real property, must the Council Member disqualify herself from participating in the decision? (We assume your answer will be yes per 2 CCR 18702.1.)

2. If a consultant whose past 12 months purchases from the Council Member exceed $250 represents a client before the City Council concerning a development proposal which will have a material financial effect upon the developer's income, assets or real property, must the Council Member disqualify herself from participating in the decision? We recognize that this answer may depend upon whether the consultant's compensation from the developer exceeds the thresholds in 2 CCR 18702.6. Please confirm our understanding in that regard. However, we do not know the terms of that contractual relationship. Does the Council Member have an obligation under the law to enquire as to the amount of compensation so as to determine the applicability of 2 CCR 18702.6?

3. Several City employees patronize the Council Member's business and spend over $250.00 in the 12 months preceding council decisions on salaries, benefits, staffing, and other terms and conditions of employment. On occasion, these salary/benefit decisions are across the board affecting


Fair Political Practices Commission  
June 17, 1992  
Page 2

all City employees equally (thus invoking the "public generally" exception), but on occasion some job classifications may be considered for special survey or merit-based adjustments. If a potential beneficiary of such across the board or special adjustment has purchased over $250 in merchandise from the Council Member during the preceding 12 months, must the Council Member disqualify herself from participating in the decision?

We would appreciate your response at your earliest opportunity.

Very truly yours,

John Torrance  
City Attorney

JT:ldc

cc: City Council  
    City Manager
